important.issue: R8 (swipe-down to dismiss) is not implemented for presentation==='sheet'. Spec §1.3 R8 and §2.3/T1.5 mandate a `PanResponder` that closes the sheet when the user drags past 50% of its height. The container only animates entrance — there is no `PanResponder` block, no `onMoveShouldSetPanResponder`, no `onPanResponderRelease`. The message-context-menu route renders a handle bar as a visual swipe affordance, but no gesture is wired to it. AC list does not explicitly test R8, which lets the gap slip through.
important.suggestion: Add a `PanResponder` to `BottomSheetContainer` when `presentation === 'sheet'`, mapping vertical drag to `translateY` and firing `onBackdropPress()` (already non-blocking-gated) when release exceeds 50% of measured height. Add an `__tests__/features/chat/bottom-sheet-router/swipe-down.test.tsx` driving `PanResponder` props directly. If product confirms Q3 (no swipe for context-menu), drop R8 from the spec and remove the handle bar in `MessageContextMenuSheetContent.tsx:102` so the affordance does not lie.

important.issue: R15/R16 (focus capture & restore) not implemented. Spec §1.5 R15-R16 + §2.5 require `findNodeHandle` on the invoking element captured at OPEN, then `AccessibilityInfo.setAccessibilityFocus(savedTag)` on close. The router calls `announceForAccessibility` (R17) — good — but never captures the previously-focused element nor restores it. VoiceOver/TalkBack users will lose context after a sheet closes.
important.suggestion: Inside `useBottomSheetRouter().open()`, capture `findNodeHandle(<currently focused node>)` via the `AccessibilityInfo.getAccessibilityFocusElement` (Android) / iOS equivalent, store the tag in the store alongside the route, and in the close-listener (`subscribeBottomSheetClose`) call `AccessibilityInfo.setAccessibilityFocus(savedTag)` best-effort. Add an `AC5+` test that spies on `setAccessibilityFocus` after open/close round-trip. Even a best-effort implementation is closer to spec than the current zero.

important.issue: R12 sequential transition is collapsed into a synchronous reducer chain. `dispatchBottomSheetEvent` auto-fires `OPEN_DONE` and `CLOSE_DONE` in the same tick (lines 62-84), so when `open('summary')` is called over an active context-menu the state goes `open(ctx) → closing(ctx, queued=summary) → opening(summary) → open(summary)` synchronously. The `BottomSheetContainer` then mounts the new content immediately and replays its enter animation while the old content was never given the chance to play its exit anim. Spec R12 explicitly says: 'play the close animation first, then mount the next sheet'. The visual effect today is a content swap, not a chained close→open.
important.suggestion: Either (a) decouple OPEN_DONE / CLOSE_DONE from the dispatcher and fire them from `BottomSheetContainer` after `Animated.timing(...).start(({finished}) => …)` resolves — restoring the spec's animation→state coupling; or (b) explicitly document in the spec that R12 is now best-effort and the sync chain is intentional. Option (a) is the spec-aligned path. Option (b) requires a spec patch and an acceptance criterion update.

important.issue: Dead expression `{blocking ? null : null}` with a comment explaining the prop is intentionally unused. The `blocking` prop is destructured but never branches behaviour — the variants happen at the router level. This is exactly the 'zombie stub' the `feedback_bury_dead_code` doctrine forbids: the comment says 'we forward it for future variants' which is YAGNI / DEPRECATED-marker territory in disguise.
important.suggestion: Drop the `blocking` prop from `BottomSheetContainerProps`, the destructure, and the dead JSX line. If a future variant (e.g. dimming intensity) needs it, it can be added back in that PR. Per doctrine: 'Live or revert'.

nice-to-have.issue: Module-level mutable `state` and `listeners` set. Survives jest test isolation only because router-level tests call `installMockRoutes()` and the router itself runs `resetBottomSheetState()` on unmount (line 50 of BottomSheetRouter.tsx). If a test suite ever renders the router twice without unmounting between, state will leak. The spec §2.6 mentioned a Context-scoped state explicitly.
nice-to-have.suggestion: Either move to `React.createContext` + provider as the spec suggested (cleaner test isolation, no global leak), or add a `beforeEach(resetBottomSheetState)` to `test-harness.tsx` and document the global as deliberate. The provider path matches the spec text; the current module-global path adds risk for a marginal win in 'no provider boilerplate'.

nice-to-have.issue: Extra indirection layer not described in spec §2.2. The lazy require is justified (`react-native-webview` native module not linked in jest), but this file is a third 'browser' shell — alongside `routes.ts` and the real `InAppBrowserSheetContent`. Spec said routes.ts directly references the content; the current path is `routes.ts → LazyInAppBrowserSheetContent.tsx → require('@/shared/ui/InAppBrowserSheetContent')`.
nice-to-have.suggestion: Acceptable if test ergonomics actually require it (verified — jest test passes only because the lazy require defers the WebView import). Document briefly in `routes.ts` (one comment near the import) why the `browser` route alone routes through a lazy wrapper, so future maintainers don't re-extract.

nice-to-have.issue: The integration test mocks the bottom-sheet-router barrel entirely and asserts against `mockRouterOpen.mock.calls` instead of rendering the real router. Spec §3 T8.2 explicitly preferred 'le rendu réel pour ne pas perdre la couverture d'intégration'. The current shape works but loses the integration coverage between the screen's effects and the router's state machine.
nice-to-have.suggestion: Optional follow-up: add a single end-to-end test that renders the real `BottomSheetRouter` + `ChatSessionScreen` together (similar to integration.test.tsx but with the real screen), so refactors of the wiring don't both pass mocks and fail real wiring.

summary: C4 livre une architecture propre: state machine pure testée, routes typées, 7 surfaces overlay migrées, 8 fichiers legacy supprimés au même commit, package.json inchangé, 107 tests verts (19 routeur + 38 SheetContent + 50 integration chat-session-deep). Lint + tsc PASS. Gates Musaium tous au vert (0 emoji unicode, 0 feature flag, 0 zombie, 8 locales i18n complètes pour les nouvelles clés a11y). 4 EARS sur 21 sont incomplets ou manquants — R8 (swipe-down), R15/R16 (focus capture/restore), R12 (séquentialité visuelle anim → state). Le store global mutable au lieu du Context spec §2.6 est pragmatique mais risqué. Un débris mineur (BottomSheetContainer ligne 147) viole feedback_bury_dead_code. Verdict CHANGES_REQUESTED: pas de blocker (rien ne casse en runtime), mais 4 important findings doivent être adressés pour atteindre la conformité spec — soit en codant R8/R12/R15/R16, soit en patchant la spec si produit valide les omissions (Q3 ouvre la porte pour R8 / context-menu).
